-module_files(solve).
-export([solve/2]).

%% Helper function to calculate XOR sum of a list
xor_sum(List) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, X) -> Acc xor X end, 0, List).

%% Main solver function
solve(Nums, Target) ->
    Length = length(Nums),
    
    % Iterate through all possible subsets using bitmasks from 0 to 2^N - 1
    % where N is the length of Nums.
    % The bitmask 'i' represents a subset: if the j-th bit of 'i' is 1, 
    % then Nums[j] is included in the subset.
    
    % We want to minimize the number of removed elements, which is equivalent 
    % to maximizing the size of the included subset (Length - removals).
    % Since we are looking for the MINIMUM removals, we iterate on the 
    % number of elements *kept* (k) starting from Length down to 0.
    % Removals = Length - k.
    
    % The problem asks for minimum removals. Minimum removals = Length - Maximum kept size.
    % We iterate over the size of the kept subset, k, from Length down to 0.
    
    case search_by_size(Length, Nums, Target) of
        {0, Removals} -> Removals;
        _ -> -1
    end.

%% Search for the minimum number of removals by checking subset sizes
search_by_size(MaxN, Nums, Target) ->
    % Iterate k (size of subset) from MaxN down to 0.
    % The first k for which a subset exists that XORs to Target yields the minimum removals (MaxN - k).
    lists:foldl(fun(Found, K) ->
        case Found of
            {_, Removals} -> Found; % Already found a solution, keep it
            _ -> 
                case find_subset_xor(K, Nums, Target) of
                    true -> {K, MaxN - K}; % Found a subset of size K
                    false -> Found % No subset of size K worked
                end
        end
    end, {null, -1}, 0..MaxN).

%% Checks if there exists a subset of size K in Nums whose XOR sum is Target.
find_subset_xor(K, Nums, Target) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    
    % Iterate over all combinations of size K. This is equivalent to iterating 
    % over all bitmasks 'i' where the number of set bits (population count) is K.
    
    lists:foldl(fun(Found, Mask) ->
        case Found of
            true -> true; % Already found a solution
            false ->
                % Check if the mask has K set bits (i.e., size K)
                case bit_count(Mask) of
                    Size when Size == K ->
                        % Construct the subset based on Mask
                        Subset = [N !! I || I <- 0..N-1, bit_test(Mask, I)];
                        
                        % Calculate XOR sum
                        XORSum = xor_sum(Subset);
                        
                        if XORSum == Target -> true;
                        true -> false % Continue searching
                    true -> false
                end
        end
    end, false, 0..(2^N - 1)).

% Bit counting helper (equivalent to population count)
bit_count(0) -> 0;
bit_count(N) -> 1 + bit_count(N rem 2) when N div 2 rem 2 == 1;
bit_count(N) -> bit_count(N div 2).

% Bit testing helper
bit_test(Mask, I) ->
    (Mask div (2^I)) rem 2 == 1.

    L_size = N div 2,
    L = lists:nth(1, Nums, 1, L_size),

-module(solver).

%% solve(list[int], int) -> int
solve(Nums, Target) ->
    Length = length(Nums),
    % Iterate through all 2^N subsets, where N is the length of Nums.
    % The subsets are represented by integers from 0 to 2^N - 1.
    % If the j-th bit of i is set, it means Nums[j] is included in the subset.
    % We want the subset whose XOR sum is Target, and whose size is maximized.
    % Maximizing the size of the remaining subset is equivalent to minimizing the number of removed elements.
    
    MaxKept = -1,

    % Loop through all 2^Length possible combinations (subsets)
    do_loop(0, 2^Length - 1, function(i) ->
        % Calculate XOR sum and count of the current subset represented by i
        {XorSum, Count} = calculate_subset_xor_and_count(Nums, i),
        
        if XorSum == Target ->
            MaxKept = max(MaxKept, Count)
        end;
        true
    end),

    if MaxKept == -1 ->
        -1; % Target is unreachable
    else
        Length - MaxKept % Minimum removed = Total - Maximum kept
    end.

%% Helper function to calculate XOR sum and size of the subset defined by the mask i
calculate_subset_xor_and_count(Nums, Mask) ->
    XorSum = 0,
    Count = 0,
    Length = length(Nums),
    
    do_loop(0, Length - 1, function(j) ->
        if bitshiftest(Mask, j, 0) =:= 1 ->
            XorSum = xor(XorSum, element(Nums, j)),
            Count = Count + 1;
            true
        end;
        true
    end),
    {XorSum, Count}.

%% Custom do_loop implementation (since Erlang standard library doesn't have a generic for-loop)
do_loop(Start, End, Fun) when Start =< End ->
    Fun(Start),
    do_loop(Start + 1, End, Fun);
do_loop(_, _, _).

%% Simple max function
max(A, B) when A >= B -> A;
max(A, B) -> B.
